name: Deep-Fried Bean Curd Stir-Fried with Mushroom
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
2 tbsp Light soy sauce
1 tbsp Hoisin sauce
2 tsp Chili sauce
1 tsp Sugar
1. Cut the bean curd into 1-inch by 1-1/2-inch flat pieces.
2. Deep-fry the bean curd pieces in hot oil for 2 to 2-1/2 minutes, until the surfaces are slightly firm and yellow. Drain thoroughly.
3. Soak the mushrooms in boiling water for about half an hour. Drain them.
4. Discard the mushroom stems and cut the caps into halves.
5. Cut the onion into thin slices.
6. Break the broccoli into individual florets and cut the stem into 1-inch wedges.
7. Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or wok over medium heat.
8. Add the onion, ginger, broccoli stems, and mushrooms to the pan. Stir-fry for about 2 minutes to season the oil.
9. Add the broccoli and vegetarian stock to the pan. Continue stir-frying for another 1-1/2 minutes.
10. Uncover the pan or wok and pour in the sauce ingredients (soy sauce, hoisin sauce, chili sauce, and sugar). Stir everything together.
11. Continue stir-frying all ingredients together for an additional 1-1/2 minutes.
12. Serve with rice and other dishes.
Source: New Chinese Vegetarian Cooking by Kenneth Lo
Typed for you by Karen Mintzias
